Which makes me all the more curious. What made him decide to face such a dangerous foe by himself?
And... why him?
Why kill such an ordinary man, one already resigned to his own mediocrity?
But he wasn't an ordinary man!
He was a hero! He came here to save lives... *sob*
Do you need a handkerchief?
I'm sorry... I was so stupid... I'm the one who got him killed...
He told me to run, so I ran. But I should've known they wouldn't let him live!
Who exactly are 'they'?
I'm not so sure myself, all I know is that they all wear masks and call themselves the 'Echoes of Herkunftshorn.'
...The name of the Witch King.
I've read that his followers believe that using his name grants them his wisdom and insight, even after his death.
That's not just a legend! Didn't you see the statue they made from Loris's flesh? They're punishing traitors in his name!
They said that the soul of the traitor will lead them to the lost Genesis Horn.
The statue in the park comes alive.
It stands up, swinging its stone instrument like an executioner's axe, bringing it down on the people below.
Ah—!
Look out!
Th-That was close... did something crush the statue?! It's so dark...
...It's nothing.
Just never speak that name to anyone ever again.
Herk... Herkun... could the legends really be true?
Is it true that His Majesty isn't really dead, and that we still live under his gaze?
Try to forget everything you saw and heard these past few days... other than Herr Bordin's sacrifice.
As an Empresses' Voice, I promise you that his death will not fade into silence.
Follow the light. My candle will protect you.
...The lost Genesis Horn? I've heard rumors.
Before he jumped from his spire following his liege's death, the Witch King's concertmaster claimed that Herkunftshorn... that the Genesis Horn still 'stands' somewhere on this land.
At first, Their Majesties believed this to refer to the Spire of the Witch King.
But that Spire had long since been toppled.
Indeed. Their Majesties destroyed every brick and tile that could have contained the Witch King's Arts, and recombined them to form the bones and blood of the Twin Empresses' Spires.
The remnants believe that the Spire still exists, then?
Or maybe it's not a spire at all.
In Leithanien, a spire is simply a symbol of one prowess in Arts.
If your theory is correct, and there's some kind of ritual behind these deaths...
Then they must be looking for the final secret the Witch King left in this realm.
...'Death of the Witch King.'
The theme of Frau Seemann's final painting.
The Witch King's remnants do not pick their victims at random. Perhaps the secret they're looking for has something to do with her painting.
Their Majesties' casters have examined all the artwork in the museum and found nothing of note.
Perhaps she left other clues in the creation process.
Frau Löwenstein, please proceed to Frau Seemann's residence. I'll meet you there.
You're going to walk the streets, just like that? Don't you know that the Gendarmerie are looking for you?
It is more efficient to find Arturia before they catch up.
You don't even care how big of a mess you make, do you? Just don't leave the cleanup to me.
You said we shared a common goal.
I suppose.
And you said that Arturia lured you away to target that officer.
But why? Why give him a chance to fight back if she just wanted him dead?
Is murder not enough for her? Does she enjoy watching people die in pain?
No, she takes no joy in hurting others.
Are you sure about that? She's hanging out with the Witch King's remnants. Why would you expect her to have basic morals?
Define morals.
Huh?
If morals are a set of behavioral rules that ensure social order, then yes, Arturia is certainly a dangerous criminal with no regard for morality.
However, if they represent the impetus and capacity to understand and respect the emotions and experiences of another, then I have never met an individual with more morals than her.
...Get to the point.
Arturia did not intend to kill Loris Bordin.
That statue was an affront to life. She would have deemed it unbearably ugly.
What's the commotion this time? Have those ignorant peasants come to cause trouble again?
Why do we give the Gendarmerie so many favors if they can't even do their jobs? Get me on the line with Bordin.
What did you say? Bordin's dead?
I knew the Gendarmerie were trash!
This cello... not too bad. It sounds just like the one from a few days ago. No doubt this culturally rich alley has drawn the attention of a famous musician.
Wings and a halo? My goodness, it's a Sankta from Laterano!
I dread for the future of our dear Leithanien!
This piece is dedicated to the return of the hero, Loris Bordin.
The tune of the cello echoes through the alley.
The moment the hero of days past rediscovered his courage, his suppressed love and hatred, regret and determination, hope and despair—all are revealed by its melody.
It is the sound of mourning, of remembrance, of praise.
The life that should have faded is brought back more brilliant than ever. Brighter than the morning sun, brighter than the blooming flowers.
Frau Seemann's belongings have been sorted and laid out upstairs... Frau Löwenstein? Are you crying?
I think I'm 'seeing' memories.
Are the marigolds in the alley blooming?
Yes. This was her favorite season. She used to lean by the window, painting the passersby.
Yes, I remember.
I loved her paintings. My memories of them have never faded in color.
Those golden days, when we all lived with hope for the future...
But before you know it, it's all in the past.
Take me to Frieda's room, Anna.
Hello.
You're back, Frau Droste. Of all the nobles I've met, I've never known a more avid reader.
I would like to access the library at the top floor.
The top floor? The forbidden section?
I need to review the records of the forbidden research conducted by the Witch King's remnants in recent years.
That might be an issue...
I can get Her Majesty's permission immediately, if necessary.
There's no need for that. Naturally, the forbidden section is open to an Empresses' Voice. However, I'm afraid that all the documents were removed this morning.
...May I ask who did it?
A spire caster. On Her Majesty's orders, as far as I know.
But it was Her Majesty who gave me her blessing to come here...
As you well know, there is more than one woman who bears that title.
I hope you're ready. I won't tolerate any more interruptions to the plan.
Almost there. I just need these experiment notes.
You Leithanians have such awful hobbies. It's such a bother trying to sift something useful from all the chaff.
Gertrude Strollo was simply a convenient woman to set up to take the fall. Her talents were mediocre.
Did you involve every noble in this? Even the ones who had secret dealings with the Victorian dukes last time?
Every one I could find.
A familiar tone.
No wonder they fear you so much, Your Majesty. Do you know what they call you behind closed doors? 'The next Witch King.' I have to say, your similar taste in wardrobe does you no favors either.
I've never denied what Herkunftshorn's intelligence, skill, and strength meant to Leithanien.
In fact, that's exactly what we need right now.
The Kurfürsten wouldn't like the sound of that at all.
It's not the title of 'Witch King' that they fear. What they fear is Lieselotte and I walking Herkunftshorn's old path, and encroaching on their power and interests.
You remind me of old Leopold. When the two of you were born, he asked me what the life expectancy of children born of the eldritch arts was.
And your answer?
I said, 'I can't give you a precise answer, Your Excellency the Großherzog, but I'm sure they'll outlive you.'
I doubt he was happy to hear that.
Was I wrong? He wanted to see the fall of Herkunftshorn, but forgot to take his own age into account.
Leopold participated in the Battle of the Four Emperors, and led the revolution that overthrew the Witch King. He was undoubtedly a hero of Leithanien.
Heroes can be greedy too.
Greed can be forgiven, as long as they stand for a unified Leithanien.
Otherwise, the greedy must be parted from their positions. Or their heads from their necks.
Ruling with an iron fist, I see. But what does Ewigegnade think about that?
Lieselotte does not approve of my methods. She whispers in my ear that such radical measures only create more opposition.
Hmph. She thinks herself the gentle mother.
Your words would sting her.
Grimmacht, my harsh Kaiserin, it is so unsettling to see such sentimentality on your face.
Lieselotte truly loves Leithanien and its people. As such, she is destined to stand in my way.
Good. Now there's the dark, brooding look I know and love.
Did you want to convince me that you'll be able to see it through when the time comes?
Many scholars believe that those born from Arts, like us, are born without a soul.
Tsk.
They say the same of you. They believe that we are of the same kind.
Oh? For our lack of souls, or for our cruelty?
Help me one more time, Fremont.
We must find the Witch King's legacy.
(Indistinct voice)
Someone... calling to me?
Was it... you?
Where's the one who saved my life?
Dead.
I'm sorry... Once again, I've let the courage of another go to waste.

...Nngh!
Water?
You poured water on my face?
Drink.

You're mistaken if you think I'll simply cooper—
Ugh!
If you won't drink, I'll have to take other measures.
*cough* *cough* Is this... medicine? Or some novel form of torture, Herr Executioner?
Dying is easy, especially for a frail, Infected noble.
But you have only one chance to do it.
Before you die, think carefully about how you really want to make your curtain call.
The sound I heard when I was passed out...
It was you, sharpening your sword.
It got some dirt on it on the way back.
It had better be sharp enough.
My neck is sturdier than you might think.
That's not for you to decide.
Hah... Are we on top of a spire?
I'm not going to let you jump off, if that's what you mean. As I said, there are no second chances.
It almost sounds like you want me to live.
It doesn't matter what I want. It's my responsibility.
You also have own responsibilities, don't you? You returned to Leithanien. Don't run away again, like a child.
Perhaps your employer forgot to mention this, but Graf Urtica is dead.
But the Urtica Grafschaft still exists.
You're still the last of House Urtica.
But I never wanted—
Running away doesn't make problems disappear.
No one can choose their birth. In that sense, we're all the same.
Is that what you tell yourself to justify serving as an executioner for that evil old fart? That you had no choice but to become his follower?
Nein. It's how I remind myself what it took to gain the freedom I have now.
What?
Using the name Urtica, the one you call an 'evil old fart' changed my family's destiny.
Everything I do today, I do to protect the hard-earned freedom of myself and many like me.
You're... thanking the Witch King?!
I'm just stating a fact.
A 'fact,' huh?
What about the 'fact' that he spilled so much blood? What about the 'fact' that his remnants have claimed so many innocent victims?
Did you think you could ignore all the pain he inflicted on others, just because of some convenient 'fact'?!
The Witch King is dead. Let the historians, politicians, and everyone else with too much time on their hands judge his deeds.
I'm a warrior. My only concern is the battle in front of me, and the battle to come.
If he truly left anything behind, then whether his legacy is good or bad isn't up to him anymore. It's up to those who make use of it.
As his heir, that's how you should be thinking.
Don't you dare look at me like that!
Your wounds are going to open up.
I don't need sympathy from a remnant of the Witch King!
Graf—
Not another word!
Ebenholz.
Which of us do you really think isn't brave enough to face the 'facts' of the Witch King?
That'll do, Lessing.
...Welcome back.
Are the Arts ready?
Arts...?
Hopefully what's in your head will come in useful, lamb of Urtica.
Now, let me see it—
Argh!
The melody is surprisingly intact, considering Leithanians know nothing of souls.
Sometimes I wonder if these people meticulously recorded even what Herkunftshorn mumbled in his sleep...
I almost miss those metaphors of his, when he used to curse out his tutor.
Y-You knew the old fart? Who are you?
Old fart? Is that how you talk about the elders of your own family? I thought you Leithanians were all about etiquette and manners. Well, that's the one thing you have in common with him, I suppose.
I have NOTHING in common with him!
Stop screaming. Are you a three-month old baby? Crying any louder won't make anyone listen.
Why don't you do something useful with your mouth? I could teach you some vocal Arts that can be cast with your tongue alone.
I'm not taking lessons from a minion of the Witch King!
A ball of Arts? Was that what hit you the last time, Lessing?
I was careless.
How many times do I have to tell you: You need to do more than swing your sword around! Train your Arts perception! I hope you never tell anyone I raised you, you stupid sheep!
I never have.
As for you, I suppose it's noteworthy that you can manage that without an Arts unit.
But your manipulation of energy couldn't be more clumsy. Are you trying to blow yourself up? Who taught you to use your lesions like that? The imbeciles in the spires?
It's all the Witch King's—
Fools. They learned from Herkunftshorn's madness, but without a sliver of his skill.
Mmph—Argh!
That's better, my little lamb. It's best that you stay put, for everyone's safety.
Check the defensive enchantment, Lessing.
Professor Hoffmann is on his way. You should wait until he arrives.
We need to get this over with quickly, before we draw the attention of the Empresses' Voice and the Gesatzswächter.
Fine. Gerhard won't be of much help, but it's not the worst thing for him to learn a lesson or two.
The Genesis Horn...
Hmph. How long as it been?
Karl Schmidt Street.
That place again? It's just an old street where a bunch of failed artists make their homes. I've already investigated it more than a few times.
Loris Bordin received a phone call before his death.
It was from an old baron by the name of Lukas, who lives on Karl Schmidt Street. Apparently, he's called for the Gendarmerie many times because of noise in the streets.
The old nobles are all like that, overreacting to the smallest things. They think they can order the people down here around like servants, even though they've been kicked out of their spires.
What I mean is, I wouldn't take it too seriously.
Loris Bordin's actions indicate the likelihood of a plausible lead there.
...Were you even listening? Just because he went there doesn't mean he thought it was worth investigating.
He may have been the commander of the Gendarmerie, but he was just a Vizegraf, and a houseless outsider at that.
Twenty-year-old honor made him little more than a glorified servant for the great nobles of a place like Zwillingstürme.
He answered the Baron's call, not because it was a useful lead, but because he had to.
This information has little relevance to the objective.
Hey, don't just go! At least—wait up!
Hm?
That shop.
'Frau Schultz's Fine Hats'? I don't usually wear hats because of my halo.
Just go in.
Good afternoon.
Good afternoon, meine Dame.
Oh... By your appearance, I take it you're a local?
You could say that.
Do you know how I can get to Karl Schmidt Street?
Hm...
I'm sorry, I'm new around here. I don't suppose young nobles like yourself leave their spires very often. I'll ask someone else.
No need. Just follow this street until the fourth statue, then make a left. You'll see it in the distance.
See what in the distance?
You didn't know? Karl Schmidt Street is also known as Marigold Alley.
Marigold...
It's where the great composer Karl Schmidt once lived.
He wrote some famous pieces about marigolds under the sun, making the alley a kind of holy ground for art lovers.
Interesting. I've never read about that.
A little anecdote, the kind you hear all over Zwillingstürme. Hardly even a story.
Well, danke. For the directions and the story.
Incidentally, you're not old enough to be buying a graduation cap, are you?
Oh, I'm waiting for someone.
If we happen to be heading to the same place... would you like to come with me?
...Thank you for the offer.
But the music of the Kaiserinnen will guide me.
Very well.
May the melody of Zwillingstürme be ever long and winding, like the rivers and the clouds.
...You can come out now.
Explain your actions.
That was an Empresses' Voice who just passed by.
She seemed decent enough, but unfortunately we're not walking the same path.
You're a little later than we agreed on, Viviana.
I met... a remarkable young man on the streets.
Now I'm sure we're not the only ones looking into this case.
You sound a little sad about that.
Ja. The spy was practically a child.
And...
You stopped. What's the matter?
I'm listening.
The music of the Kaiserinnen is still playing.
Zwillingstürme still bathes in their wonderful melody.
But... can a melody played by divergent wills remain in harmony forever?
You fear the Empresses' Voice. Is your employer an enemy of the Empresses?
Are you out of your mind? Don't ask that question out on the street!
We'll be entering the alley soon. I will ask then.
Fine. You want to know if I'm in league with the people who killed Loris Bordin? Absolutely not.
I'm a Lupo, which I would hope is obvious. I was born near Siracusa.
In the early years of the Witch King's reign, not long after Siracusa broke away from Leithanien, how do you think he and his followers treated my people?
I was inquiring about you personally, not the history of your family.
Is there a difference?
I had assumed that you made your own choices of your own free will.
Free will? Not in my family. I was born to carry our destiny.
Just like the lady who recently became an Empresses' Voice. She went from Leithanien to Kazimierz, and from Kazimierz back to Leithanien. Was any of that her own choice?
I am inferring that it's complicated. This will make it difficult to discern friend from foe.
Hah... Why do you think they call Laterano Paradise?
Stop.
What's the matter? Karl Schmidt Street is just ahead. The Empresses' Voice went there. Maybe Bordin really did find something.
The alley has changed from before.
It is too quiet.
Are these pieces all by Frau Seemann?
Ja, meine Dame.
All drafts of Der Tod des Hexenkönigs.
I didn't dare touch them, nor did I understand them, so I left them where they were.
What about anything else from the past few years?
The past few years... There's not much to show. She often said that her passion ended that autumn, twenty-three years ago. Would you like to peruse her earlier works?
If you don't mind.
Frau Seemann's early works are characterized by refined touches and bright, lively colors.
Frieda Seemann wanted to capture authentic emotions with her brush. She had an excellent memory, and made good use of Originium Arts to recreate snapshots in time.
She was still young when I saw her in the Witch King's Spire for the first time.
She had an almost Minoan liveliness to her, less of the stiffness of your average Leithanian noblewoman.
Her easy-going nature attracted people from all walks of life.
Frieda's friends used to hold poetry gatherings here in this alley... Your parents were among them.
My parents? They came here too?
Did they never tell you? This is where they met.
Your father wasn't a Kurfürst-in-waiting quite yet. He came from Sturmland to Einwald, and often participated in gatherings of young people in Marigold Alley.
One day, a young woman of common stock mustered the courage to read a little poem she wrote at Frau Seemann's poetry gathering.
And that was my mother?
She never told me any of this.
Her brow's always been furrowed, for as far back as I can remember...
Lucinda Droste was a shy, gentle girl with soft, gleaming eyes. Gazing upon her was like sitting by the lakeside in summer, with a gentle breeze brushing by.
Werner fell in love at first sight, almost missing His Majesty's summons.
If Brandt hadn't covered for him, he would have been part of a statue in front of the Spire by the time House Hochberg's soldiers arrived at Einwald.
This was still during the reign of the Witch King?
Hard to believe, isn't it?
But that era was resplendent in its own way, much like the painting before you.
A resplendent... marigold.
This painting looks familiar. I think I've seen it before.
But where?

lines.st: 'Viviana.'
'I'm sorry, but I need to take this painting away.'
'Your mother is gone. I'll have someone clear out the room.'
'I couldn't possibly leave you here with no one to take care of you.'
'Someone will be here to take you away soon.'
'Don't cry. You'll meet new friends out there. There will be people who love you, even without Mutti and Vati.'
'You'll see the marigolds with your own eyes.'
'See for me if they're blooming, like in the painting.'

lines.x: ...A flower chasing the sun.
The clouds above Sturmland rarely clear up. It was cold and wet up in the spire. Father tried many things, and hired many casters, but could never get them to survive in the flower pots.
But here in this tiny Zwillingstürme alley, they're everywhere.
It's... warm.
Where are you going, Viviana?
...An enemy?
Viviana!
The music is faint, almost imperceptible.
It seems distant, but also nearby.
It transcends time, transcends memories, linking them in the same space.
You're going back.

lines.st: Ernst's condition deteriorated rapidly. Even the best healers the Twins could find couldn't save his life.

lines.x: Which means you could be the new Kurfürst of Sturmland.

lines.st: When I first came here, I thought I was going to be a poet.

lines.x: What about Lucinda?

lines.st: I certainly hope she'll come with me, but I won't force her. She's talented. She could become a great painter under Frau Seemann's tutelage.

lines.x: You love her, and she loves you.

lines.st: Which is why I can't lie to her.
I'm no longer the carefree youngest son of the Hochbergs. From this day forth, my life belongs to Sturmland and to House Hochberg. Not to myself.
The nobles will have a whole gaggle of young ladies lined up, all vying to be my wife.
I wouldn't be able to promise her anything, even if she were willing to come. If we lose the power struggle, she could end her days as a handmaiden in our spire.

lines.x: His Majesty... the Witch King is dead. The Spire that towered over Leithanien has fallen. Our days of being slaves to fate were expected to be left in the past.

lines.st: At the very least, we now know that the name of our fate isn't 'the Witch King.'

lines.x: Just give it one last try, won't you? Either take her with you, or stay here for her.
Just like the night when the Witch King fell, I pray that the kind and gentle will have their happy ending.
The music grows clearer.
Unlike the performance of the statues, this music is alive.
Like a kindly hand, it pulls her forward, down the stairs, away from the wind and rain.
It is close. Almost there. Down the stairs, and the sunlight is waiting outside—
The car is here.

lines.st: Lucinda is not here.
I must find her.

lines.x: Everyone is waiting for you.
The Witch King is dead. Your men must return to their lands, in accordance with our agreement with the Twins.
You must leave the city before noon. The armies of the other Kurfürsten are watching your every move.
Sturmland... Nein, the fate of Leithanien falls on your shoulders.

lines.st: When did you become such a stranger, Brandt?
You always wear that helmet. I can't see your eyes. Sometimes, I can't even tell if it's you speaking, or another Gesatzswächter.

lines.x: Because it makes no difference whether it's me or another Gesatzswächter.
Leithanien cannot afford another civil war. Sturmland needs a Kurfürst who the people will get behind.

lines.st: You're not here to protect me, are you?
Just like you didn't protect Ernst or father that night.

lines.x: ...Do you remember, Werner? When I was selected for Gesatzswächter training, I came to you and Ernst and told you I wanted to stay.

lines.st: You grew up with us and swore fealty to House Hochberg. You didn't want to go back on your vow.

lines.x: But you told me—
'You're defending something far more important than any person's life, more important than House Hochberg.'
I never forgot that. Not even for a moment.

lines.st: Alright then.
Is fate truly so impossible to resist, Brandt?

lines.x: No, don't go.
Don't give up hope.
At least, not so easily...
The young Kurfürst turns around and leaves, but Viviana does not want to follow. She tries her hardest to go back.
A golden glow always fills the alley in autumn. Her mother must be somewhere in there, simply unable to make it in time.
She runs, she searches.
Marigolds bloom under the sun, just like the hopes her parents once had for the future.
She wants to grab ahold of her mother, to grab ahold of her father's silhouette from the memory.
As though she could change something if she did.
Change the tragedy that was her father's life... and change her own.
Year after year, the golden flowers take in the city's joy, anger, and sorrow, remaining as bright as ever in bloom.
But some blooming hopes were left behind forever, twenty-three years ago.
Why...
Why couldn't I grab it?
Is fate truly so impossible to resist?
Viviana! Around you!
Viviana looks up in a trance.
The music stops.
The melody seems to freeze in place, then becomes rushing energy.
Like dark clouds, the Arts tear the sunlight apart and rush toward her.
Ugh—!
No need to fear, I'm here with you.
A cello...
Mental Arts.
The music is awakening our memories of Werner.
Mother... Father...
Over here, Viviana. I'll cover your ears with my hands.
They're... gone...
I couldn't change anything...
Don't listen to it. Whatever you're seeing in your imagination isn't happening in the present.
The remnants of the Witch King are in this alley. They knew we were coming.
Ugh... there are too many of them.
I can't deal with them all. We need to escape—
Another sound emerges.
It is like a melody, but cannot be sung. It is like a bell, but continuous, without pausing.
It is a declaration that rings in the chambers of the heart.
Like the brightest setting sun, radiant purple Arts shine through the clouds and fill the streets.
This melody... the Gesatzswächter?
Brandt? Is that you?
...Take Werner's daughter inside.
As for you, embers of evil...
Filthy dregs picked from the trash heap—
—How dare you defile Zwillingstürme!
Baron Peter Lukas turns seventy-three this year.
He inherited his title from his uncle three years ago. Six months later, he moved to Karl Schmidt Street due to the expenses of maintaining the Lukas family spire.
He enjoys Karl Schmidt's music, but hates the street.
He finds the sunlight, the flowers, the youth's graffiti, and the jazz music coming from the nearby bar unbearable.
He orders his servant to close the curtains, turns on the gramophone, and the room is immediately filled with the sound of cello.
The Sankta was a truly talented musician, he thinks. So talented, he could almost forgive her for being Lateran.
He plays eleven instruments, ten of which he learned during the reign of the Witch King.
To send the best melodies into the Genesis Spire, talented young musicians were put into a room and made to compose day and night.
He loved the violinist, her delicate fingers, and the beauty mark on the edge of her palm.
Tears flowed down his face when he visited Victoria for the first time and heard music from his homeland on the stage of the Royal Theater. He kissed her for the first time.
That was his elegant, dignified Leithanien.
He misses it.
Trying to destroy houses and escape in the chaos?
There is nowhere for you to hide before the Golden Melody.
Mmph—Argh!
You're the last.
Remove your mask and surrender to Leithanien, and you will be spared the pain of a burning soul.
This pain is... insignificant.
Have you ever heard the Witch King's Reichsorchester play?
The black forests of Einwald, the lakes and ports of Fortsgard, the storms of Sturmland... anything you can imagine about Leithanien, you could feel in the melody.
The Witch King did not interpret or conduct. He simply walked amongst them.
As one of his musicians, you had to follow the guidance of his sightline, and keep a tight pace with him. Fall out of line, and you were ripped apart by the magnificent melody.
The Witch King's favorite punishment for those too weak to serve him.
But I kept up.
I became a part of that transcendent melody. Since then, I could no longer tolerate playing for the mediocre.
What did you think you were? Interpreters of the great melody? Ideal-driven revolutionaries?
Nein.
All I see are criminals hiding behind masks, killing the innocents of Leithanien and undermining her peace and order... Clowns, burying their faces in the embers of the Witch King.
Your life belongs to the Golden Melody now.
But I suppose you were already dead, from the moment you gave up your free will and become the Witch King's vassal.
Agh... hah... haha!
And what about you then? Aren't you wearing a mask yourself?
The Arts you use: the Golden Melody, the light of Leithanien, passed down through generations of Gesatzswächter, those who guard the Güldenesgesatz—
Are you not also a servant of a melody written by a greater will?
Even the greatest individual will is nothing before Leithanien herself.
Then...
Is it Leithanien's Golden Melody that pronounces my death sentence, or you yourself?
Is this your escape plan? Did you think the will of the Gesatzswächter would waver before such cliches?
Preposterous.
Why... would I need an escape plan?
I was... chosen to find you.
The Concertmaster wants to know.... When the events of twenty-three years ago echoed in your ears just now...
Did you see Ernst von Hochberg, for whom help would never arrive in time...?
Or his brother Werner, who died in sorrow as the Kurfürst he never wanted to be?
The Witch King cultist's body turns to dust under the power of dual Originium Arts.
Only her last words remain with the ashes.

lines.st: Brandt Reiner.
You cannot avoid a choice forever.

lines.x: On that day, twenty-three years ago, Father didn't wait for Mother.
They missed each other.
Lucinda went to the spice market before coming to the studio that morning. She wanted to try a different incense, see if it would lift her spirits.
She did not know that she was already with child.
Mother went to Sturmland in the end.
She loved you, and she loved Werner. She could not have raised you on her own if she'd stayed in Zwillingstürme.
She knew that Werner had already gotten married, but she had no other choice.
Father had a choice.
Ten minutes. If Werner had waited another ten minutes, he'd have met Lucinda and known that she was willing to face it all and fight by his side.
He... regretted those ten minutes for the rest of his life.
...I know.
He never told me, but I knew.
A marigold and the moon... it was never meant to be. It was a dream that, upon waking, they knew to be beyond reach, words spoken only to comfort.
Don't you blame your father?
I can't.
We're too alike.
You told me that he aspired to be a poet.
But he never once recited a poem in front of me.
It was only after I returned to Leithanien that I realized... When someone no longer has the strength to face reality, the illusions that once brought hope now bring only pain.
Viviana...
You've been busy since your father's passing, shuttling from Sturmland to Zwillingstürme.
All I want to say is... it's alright to express your sorrow.
I just want to rest for a while.
Just for a little while.
Cora says no more. She closes the curtains before she leaves.
The darkness wraps around Viviana like a familiar blanket. Cora knows her well, knows what brings her comfort.
She gazes upon the paintings in the room, now covered in shadow, and the golden times depicted within gaze back at her.
Fortunately, the Arts in that music didn't cause Viviana any real damage.
But they brought back memories of Werner, as they did for us. And it must be even more painful for her.
You're injured.
I am?
Oh... this wasn't from the battle. I might have bumped myself when I went downstairs chasing after Viviana.
Why not try the therapeutic machine I brought back from Columbia?
I didn't lose my eyesight yesterday, Brandt. I've managed on my own for the past twenty-some years.
You, though... something's off with your voice. Were these enemies that tough?
What an absurd notion.
That's more like you.
I wish you'd take off your helmet and catch your breath once in a while, though. Even the finest instrument succumbs to wear and tear when used nonstop.
I don't deserve to rest.
The Gendarmerie is on their way. They'll take it from here.
You should leave before they arrive.
Now? But we're so close to the truth.
Leithanien has not fallen so far as to need to entrust her future to a blind instrument tuner and a retired competition knight.
The cultists are looking for the Witch King's legacy. Frieda and Loris were witnesses to that night. Werner... House Hochberg was part of the coup as well.
If they're killing witnesses to complete some sort of ritual, they'll be looking for their next target.
Which is why you need to listen to me and stop your investigation.
What about you, Brandt? You were there on the day of the coup too...
I am a guardian of Leithanien's will. A few Witch King worshippers won't get me.
Besides, they laid an ambush for you here. You're the ones they're targeting.
But how did they know we were coming? Could Viviana really be right, and Frieda's paintings hold an important clue?
I believe so.
Herr Reiner, please allow me to continue the investigation. I want to know what Frau Seemann left behind.
You're... holding the draft to her final work.
'Regret.'
The strongest emotion and desire that the painter had ever felt in her life.
She spent her last days painting it over and over again.
Through the strokes of her brush, she wanted to see the divine in her heart.
You're saying those haphazard strokes were intentional?
Ja. It looks messy because she painted on the same sheet over and over again.
The scene of the Witch King's death is still in the draft.
Put the painting on the ground, Viviana.
Okay.
Brandt, try to 'play' it.
Tell me when to stop.
It's vibrating, like a musical instrument.
There are minute differences in each layer of paint.
I can't see, but my Arts allow me to 'hear' the frequency of each layer.
Almost there... just remove the layer of paint above, and we'll find...
The very first version.
Do you see it?
It's blurry, but I can tell that it's the interior of a spire. It's... imposing.
The Witch King's Spire. I imagine that even Frieda could only paint a small part of it.
Can you identify the figures in the painting?
There is no Witch King. The throne is empty. The battle seems to be over.
The painter herself, Frieda Seemann, is behind the curtain.
There is a Liberi soldier charging in from the hallway... Loris Bordin?
And... um...
Who is this Caprinae holding an Arts unit and cleaning up?
Gerhard Hoffmann.
One of the most talented casters in all of Einwald, formerly in service to House Urtica.
He became a teacher after the Witch King's death.
We were late.
These lunatics don't care how many innocents they kill.
*cough*... *cough*.
You're injured. I can treat your wounds.
Danke.
Where is Baron Lukas? Is he upstairs?
The Baron... didn't make it.
He'd been talking about moving for a while. We wouldn't have gotten caught up in all this if we hadn't put the plans on hold just because of that Sankta cellist playing in the area!
What did their performance sound like?
Do you want to hear it? The Baron had me record it. I wonder if the gramophone's still alright...
Huh, you actually found a clue.
Loris Bordin found it.
Is it Arturia?
Yes.
I may not be able to recognize a live performance or not, but I would never mistake her playing.
Aren't we in trouble then?!
No need to fear. There are no Arts in the recording, and she isn't nearby. Someone broadcasted her performance on this street a few minutes ago.
And her Arts worked over such a large area?
She must have accomplices. It would only be possible through a combination of Leithanian Arts and her own.
You can tell all that just from listening to this?
This section here. It's not the sound of a cello. It's a pipe organ, and one of uncommon size.
A huge pipe organ...
It's an unusual instrument. Where might you find one in Zwillingstürme?
...Ludwigs-Universität.
A school?
Ja. It used to be the spire of learning for House Urtica.
His head hurts.
He has not felt such pain since he escaped Vyseheim.
When was the last time it hurt so much that he wanted to scream, to vomit, to cry?
It was...
He could remember. He just didn't want to.
...Say something.
Gloat, why don't you? You're on the cusp of victory, after I've kept you trapped in my head for so long.
When I die... I sure hope I get a proper death.
Unlike you, who's been dead long enough to crumble to dust, only to be pieced back together and brought back to life.
What is he babbling about?
You haven't started the ritual yet, have you?
Not yet, Professor.
He looks like he's in pain. Can he really take it?
He's from Herkunftshorn's bloodline. If he can't take even this much, then Ewigegnade must have intentionally raised him to be useless.
He's suffered much for his age.
If he's unable to bear it, interrupting the performance of the Voice of Mundane could cause the Genesis Horn to go out of control and affect all of Ludwig.
I'm here. The spire will hold.
But you swore an oath to Her Majesty that you would not reveal the true extent of your powers. She'd have to answer to the nobles if the students learned of your identity.
Ludwigs-Universität may be shut down, which would affect your relocation plans.
And Ermi will give you an earful when she gets back to Leithanien.
...Bah. Keeping this boy alive is harder than summoning the Genesis Horn.
Where is Lessing?
He's standing watch outside.
He said he'll ensure that no one can enter before the ritual is complete.
Pah, nothing more than keeping his mind from being torn to pieces. Where is the device I made for those nutjobs in Bolívar?
Are you alright?
Hah...
It's okay. They've left the room.
You're...
We met not too long ago.
Forest... Park...
Ja. I wanted to warn you, but Lessing was there. I couldn't take you away with him watching.
Lessing...
He's not evil by nature, but he can be stubborn.
I used to be like him too. But all it takes to change is courage.
Can you move? Try to grab my hand.
What are you doing, Gerhard?!
I'm sorry, Professor.
I can't let you use Herkunftshorn's bloodline like this. He's a living, breathing soul, one with a bright future.
Spatial defense Arts— I taught you those!
You also taught me that even the most powerful Arts cannot erase regret.
I've caused a lot of harm with the Arts you taught me.
But when I'm alone at night, I often ask myself: how many souls would have continued to shine brightly, had I not done what I did?
Would we be living in better times? Would the people around us have been better? Would we be better ourselves?
That's why I gave up on being a spire caster and became a scholar.
What are you babbling about, Gerhard?
You're making a big mistake. Step away from the boy—
...A mistake?
Nein, Professor.
I'm saving his life. I'm not going to miss my chance this time. I'm fixing the biggest mistake that you and I... that we ever made.
...The rising of the Urticas' secret chamber destroyed the original plate of Ludwigs-Universität. Coupled with the influence of Herkunftshorn's Arts, the school can no longer function normally.
For now, the Empresses' Voice have taken over the cleanup operation from the Gendarmerie. Unaffected students and faculty have been relocated to neighboring Ostendorfhof.
The cleanup will take about a week. Thereafter, they will be able to return to campus...

No need. Ludwigs-Universität's library will be relocated to Neuleopoldshof and fall under the purview of the Reichsmuseum. The other campuses and faculties will be merged into the Reichskunstakademie in Bachshof.
The Gesatzswächter will be in charge of disposing of all of the remaining plates belonging to Urtica.
But then Ludwigs-Universität will—
Ludwigs-Universität will become history.
Your Majesty...
Are you surprised?
When I received the summon, I thought—
You thought I would be Lieselotte.
My apologies.
You did well. Lieselotte thinks very highly of you.
Danke schön, Your Majesty. If you hadn't graced us with your presence yesterday...
It's behind us, no need to speak of it again.
Your Majesty... there is one other thing.
Oh?
Arturia... the wanted Sankta, I bumped into her, and she mentioned my father.
I would like to know if she had something to do with his death. If you would permit me...
We've been over this, Hochberg.
The case of the Witch King's remains stealing a copy of the Güldenesgesatz has been closed.
Brandt.
Your Majesty, you are here as well.
Have you come to see Cora?
She is currently rehearsing the Güldenesgesatz. The delay caused by the Witch King's remains has us all worried about tomorrow's performance.
Rest assured, the Movement is flawless at all times.
Cora said the same. How long have you gone without a break? Why not take a vacation after the Celebration?
You could go back to Sturmland together. Not as a Reichsorchester tuner and Gesatzswächter, but as Cora and Brandt.
This is a reward, not a punishment. For mopping up the Witch King's remains. You and Viviana have done fine work, and I've taken notice.
Just remember that... opportunity does not last forever. Some things must be said before it's too late.
Thank you, Your Majesty, for the reminder.
And another thing, I'll need at least twenty Gesatzswächter for tomorrow's Celebration.
What?
Does Grimmacht know about this...?
Every year, Das Kaiserinnensfest begins with Reichsorchester's performance of the Güldenesgesatz, and ends with my duet with Hildegard.
I wonder.... This year, will the people still appreciate our performance?
...I have no doubt, Your Majesty.
Could I ask you a question, Brandt?
By all means.
Assume your most prized possession has a crack in it. Would you... turn a blind eye to it? Pretend you didn't notice?
You're going to leave Zwillingstürme?
Arturia is under control. I will return to Laterano and report to His Holiness the Pope. We will subsequently seek formal legal cooperation from Leithanien.
Alright. But I should remind you... tomorrow is Das Kaiserinnensfest.
Zwillingstürme will be very lively. Young people from other regions, even other countries, will all join in the merrymaking.
Correct, I had noticed an increase in foot traffic.
And so, I want to ask... Herr Executor, won't you stay just one more day?
Please explain why I'd do that.

Um... Arturia has been apprehended, the copy of the Güldenesgesatz has been recovered, and the Witch King's remains have been more or less wiped out. It seems like everything's all taken care of, but I can't shake this uneasy feeling.
Grimmacht says I have a unique sense for danger... I mean, of course, it might just be my imagination.
For matters pertaining to internal Leithanian affairs, I suggest you confer with your empress.
She's been too busy to see me today.
So that's why you have time to escort me out of the city? I had assumed this was an official duty.
A duty? No, it's not my duty to monitor you.
And, err, having come all the way to Zwillingstürme, wouldn't you like to stay a bit longer? Make some new friends? See the sights?
Friends and sights...
Are you looking at... that coffee shop?
I could try the drink Loris recommended before I leave.
H-Herr Sankta!
You are Yulia's brother, Jan. Do you work at this coffee shop?
Ja, I need to save money so I can study abroad when I'm ready. I thought I'd never see you again.
I've heard that the executors of the Lateran Notarial Hall carry out the wills of Lateran citizens, is that right?
An acceptable summary.
And you also... solve cases?
So long as it is part of a binding document, I will do everything in my power to carry it out.
Wunderbar! Loris left me a key before he died. He said there were clues about my sister's case in his house.
But I'm not a detective after all, and wasn't sure who to give the key to.
This is the residence of Loris Bordin.
This is a home in Zwillingstürme! You've barely known Bordin for an afternoon and he left you his entire house?!
What he left me are clues to a case.
Look—The wall, the table, the drawers, everywhere.
These are all clues regarding Yulia Schüler, the girl who disappeared over ten years ago.
And... a letter.
Loris claimed to have accepted defeat. But actually, he never gave up on the case.
I almost forgot that you're a case-solving machine. Even if they made you a Graf and granted you the tallest spire in Zwillingstürmeshof, you'd hardly blink, would you?
A false premise, that is. There is no precedent for a Lateran saint being conferred a title by another country.
And what do you have to do with Lateran sainthood...? Are you suggesting that you, Herr Executor, bear the title of Saint?
Yes.
Like the Founders and the Popes?
I am unclear on what you mean by 'like' in this context, but nominally yes.
...I may need to reevaluate our need to monitor you, Herr Saint.
You are an 'executor' to an empress. I had assumed you had some familiarity with my background.
I thought I did too.
Do not worry. Whether or not I hold the title of saint is not particularly relevant to my work in Leithanien.
My executorial duty is to capture Arturia, as well as executing the wills of Lateran citizens.
As the Arturia case is on temporary hold, fulfilling the will of Loris Bordin is my next objective.
Was that... hesitation? I've never seen you hesitate before!
I still have questions about the Arturia case.
What questions?
I need to think.
Federico looks at the piano to the side of the room.
Like most Leithanians, Loris had musical instruments in his home. However, judging from the many files and folders on its bench, he did not play the piano often.

Simply tap the black and white keys in a certain rhythm and a melody will be created. People have taken such immediate feedback for granted, yet there are still many questions that, once sprouted, are pushed away, unanswered.
The piece you just played... is it the one from yesterday at Ludwigs-Universität?
Der Tod des Hexenkönigs.
Arturia interpreted the emotions of Loris Bordin and Brandt Reiner, as well as the others who witnessed the Witch King's demise. She turned their strongest feelings into notes, creating this piece.
I want to know what she discovered in those emotions.
The notes may be the same, but the feelings are so different.
Herr Executor, are you and Arturia really related by blood? If misplaying an instrument were a crime, you might well be arrested as soon as you step out the door.
I did not play a single note wrong.
But there was no emotion whatsoever! You can't grasp music by memorizing its rules. You have to... um... picture the emotion it conveys.
For example, Loris Bordin. You have to become him, imagine the night he charged into the Witch King's Spire. Picture how he became a hero, how crestfallen he was by Yulia's case, and...
When you're finally forced to doubt yourself, and find yourself wavering, lost, like me... you'll realize that you need a strength, a reason.
And that's when you'll discover that the law offers no guidance.
Why do barriers never cease to be? Is mankind doomed never to understand one another?
The tiniest chaos is enough to shatter the order. The moment confusion envelops, people begin to hurt one another...

Each era has its own trials and tribulations, for which we can only prepare to the best of our ability. It is certain, however, that you have been chosen because It believes that you have the ability to respond to the unknown.
You are well aware that there is another besides yourself very unique among the Sankta.
Bring her back, if possible. I need to meet with her, face to face.
Fede, your whole life, you have been different, different from all the others.
Can you really... not see everything I see in the music?
Thanks to Grimmacht, both my ears and my soul have been saved.
A failed attempt, I take it? Let's not try that again. We have to acknowledge that some people simply lack the talent for music.
Yes, I cannot empathize with Loris or anyone else, nor do I see the illusions created by the notes.
But I do have realized that emotions are also information.
What do you mean by that?

As you said, Arturia's performances are completely different from mine. The notes arranged in order have no inherent meaning, but with added emotion, they become a piece of music beloved by you people.
And so, I have come to understand that emotion is a kind of additional information.
Arturia played her music, playing the hearts of Loris and others, to extract information from their powerful emotions that even they themselves could not retrieve.
This is something only Arturia can do.
Her objective has not yet been achieved, nor will she abandon it. There will be even greater chaos in the coming days, here in Zwillingstürme.
...Are you certain?
I could spend time explaining the finer logic to you, but I assess that you will find it difficult to share my thought process. As such, I suggest you consider my conclusion a kind of... intuition.
Herr Executor, there is someone you should see. She can help you.
Congratulations, you're about to be rid of me.

What, I shouldn't say such things? What would you rather hear? Mockery, anger, curses? Or a seemingly invincible enemy pulled straight from a story begging for mercy before their departure from the stage?
Don't forget, you wanted to see me. Whenever you have doubts, you unconsciously seek out my voice.
You want to hear me deny you, and then, in denying me, you can win some silly comfort for yourself.
It is not I who needs you. You need me.
Bloodline of Urtica, Franz, Ebenholz——Without me, what does your future hold?
It's done.
What do you mean...? What's done?
I've removed the Voice of Mundane from your head.
But...
But nothing happened, is that it? It could never summon the Genesis Horn in the first place!
It's like watching a crowd of people nervously gathering to light a fuse and blow Zwillingstürme up, only to hear a soft pop like a gloompincer's depressing fart.
But didn't you say the melody of the Witch King could open the door to the Genesis Horn?
Yes, the right melody. Do you think you can open a locked door with any old key?! Don't compare my research on Herkunftshorn with the pile of rubbish they stuffed into your brain.
Those idiots and their Infected bombing couldn't manage a thing!
Ha, 'The Voice of Mundane'... Do the people who come up with this stuff even know the difference between a proper creation and a belch?
Was it Gerhard who filled your heads with all this? Would you mind if I dug him up and knocked him around a bit more?
But they told me the Voice of Mundane housed the Witch King's avatar.
Avatar? Haha... avatar.
That'd be like me rolling my soul into a ball and tossing it into the amphitheater beneath the spire for you stupid lambs to kick about. You really think that can be done?
That's enough, boy. I'm in a bad mood, a positively rotten one. I thought by doing this one last favor for Grimmacht, I'd be able to take my things and finally be rid of this wretched place.
Who'd have thought those masked morons would make a fool of me... No, they made me a whole circus!
I should have Ermengarde look me over, see if a Lich's age has any effect on his brain.
He said... The Voice of Mundane is just noise.
It is certainly a melody left by the Witch King, that's true. But Gerhard and his men misunderstood its power. It isn't the key to the Witch King's legacy.
Then what about the... voice in my head?
I don't understand the Arts behind it.
The problem of the Voice of Mundane has been resolved. You may still suffer headaches and hallucinations, but I expect they won't be so bad going forward.
Just to be safe, you should stay here and rest a while. Then leave Zwillingstürme after Das Kaiserinnensfest.
So, the voice is just my imagination then?
All these years, and all of my struggles were just in my head? In the somewhat more figurative sense?
Apparently, I have nothing whatsoever to do with any grand plans of those lofty conspirators.
And to speak of killing myself before I went mad... Hah, I've been mad for a long, long time.
I don't have time to discuss this with you.
...Right.

You grandfather says that the rubbish in my head tainted the research on Herkunftshorn. So why care about me, an inferior product? Particularly after we've known that none of his power lies within me.
I can't summon a Catastrophe cloud with a wave of my hand, nor can I cleave a spire with a swing of a sword—
What do you want?
The power is still there. Since you've removed the Voice of Mundane, why not... kill me?
The Voice of Mundane is not the key, meaning I'm of no use to the Twin Empresses. Why not burn me and mine to ashes, like all the experiments of the Witch King era?
Because there is no...
...No need? Is that it?
Then what about my brother? He died. He died because of a stupid plan like... To borrow a phrase, like a gloompincer's depressing fart. Such nonsense... such irony!
He was grateful, you know, right up until he died... Grateful for our meeting, grateful for the instructor who taught him the cello.
But our meeting was exploited by Gertrude. The teacher he admired was mixed up with the Witch King's remains.
Hah, and now you tell me... you tell me the Voice of Mundane was all a wacky misunderstanding the entire time!
So we lived, died, met, parted, all for what? Where is the meaning in any of it?
Alright, if you want an answer, I'll tell you.
You're right. There was no meaning in any of it.
Set aside the Urtica name. Without the Voice of Mundane, you're nothing but a self-absorbed, self-harming, self-destructive empty shell of a pretty boy.
Perfect. You finally did it.
Now take up your Arts Unit.
Why not unwrap the bandages on your sword? That would make this execution go even smoother.
The man before me is unworthy of it.
Even now, you just have to humili—
Then fight back. Prove me wrong by your actions.
Ugh...
You loathe the bloodline of the Witch King. But have you ever sought out the victims of his persecution? To make amends, to help them, to compensate them?

You loathe the title of Graf Urtica. But have you ever looked into the safety of the commoners? Taken note of the decline of your domain, caught between the Twin Empresses and the Witch King's remains?
You lament being the only survivor. But you have not been cherishing the life ahead of you. Instead, you constantly throw yourself into danger.
You say you were a prisoner of your spire. But who, in the end, has never looked beyond his cage at anyone else?
Lessing's sword moves so quickly that dodging is nearly impossible. He's rarely spoken so many words in one breath.
At first, Ebenholz had no intention of dodging, but almost instinctively, the Originium dice float into the air. Arts pour out uncontrollably, like fiery rage.
Blood, title, duty, everything was forced upon me by fate.
I tried! I stood against it! And in the end, you tell me there was never any need to!
Why? Why did I have to go through all this when I'm nobody at all?
Lessing's sword presses against Ebenholz's throat. The Originium dice fall to the side.
The battle is over, the outcome a surprise to no one.
Our fight against fate never belonged to any of us. And so, what does the suffering we've endured... even count for?
I never received a noble's education, and I don't throw around words like 'fate.'
I only know that all the suffering is real.
I've seen street hawkers beaten by the Gendarmerie in the alleyways. I've seen old men freeze to death on the roadside after losing their farmland to the rural nobility.
They'll never see the Witch King, the Twin Empresses, or a Kurfürsten in their life. They'll never have the time or energy to contemplate fate, suffering, or meaning.
That's life in the shadow of the spires, for the great masses of Leithanien.
No one can overcome an abstract concept. If you want revenge, choose a concrete target. The Witch King, the Twin Empresses, the still living remnants.
We fight to survive. And we don't stop until our own blood or that of our enemies runs dry.
Fight...
The Witch King's legacy still exists, wherever it is. Enemies shrouded in darkness may find it at any moment, and use it to unleash suffering on the great masses once again.
You can stay in this spire and continue lamenting your 'fate,' or you can challenge me again and die by my hand.
Or... you can join me in seeking the Witch King's legacy. Help me find the true culprit, who may still be alive in some form.
Choose.
Ebenholz feels the sword against his throat, still wrapped in bandages. Were it not wrapped, he would have smelled blood.
Of course, this does not mean that Lessing can't kill him. The warrior's expression is cold, but his eyes burn with anger.
Why is he angry?
Why... does he still have expectations for a man like him?
Would you kindly pass me my Arts Unit?
Why aren't they here?
Frau Hochberg, what is it you're looking for?
Sketches... Frieda Seemann's drawings.
Since Herr Reiner and I left, has anyone else been in this room?
Um... no suspicious individuals, meine Dame.
After you and Herr Gesatzswächter left, the Imperial casters carefully inspected the entire alley.
After confirming no traces of the Witch King's remains, and no dangerous witchcraft or items, they left.
Are the drawings you're looking for involved in some important case?
A case?
Viviana recalls Grimmacht's words.
'The case of the Witch King's remains stealing a copy of the Güldenesgesatz has been closed.'
No.
It's merely personal. Frau Seemann's work is incredibly beautiful. It always evokes some... marigold memories in me.
Please don't alert the others though. I don't want too many people to know I've been here.
Of course! Rest assured. The Gendarmerie will never disclose your movements.
So lovely. Marigolds, sunlight... such beautiful things tend to inspire creativity and nurture emotion.
No wonder so many Leithanian artists love this alley. Werner von Hochberg... He was obsessed with it until the day he died.
Might you be... Frau Giallo? Weren't you taken away by Grimmacht's casters?
I'm certainly not here.
And you didn't really bump into your father back there either.
This is all in your imagination.
Is this also an effect of your Arts?
You're rather skilled in Originium Arts yourself. What do you think?
I... haven't noticed anything unusual.
People often misunderstand my Arts. The truth is, I can't do very much at all. People are just ignorant of their own hearts. Or even if they do know, they're unwilling to face themselves.
I told you the first time we met. My melodies resonate with the emotions deep inside the human heart... and once they ripple, they are hard to ignore.
It's you who wants to change your father's fate. The one talking to you now, well, I'm afraid it's you yourself.
...Is that so?
You yearn to know the cause of your father's death.
You fear that the conspiracy of the Witch King's remains is not yet through. You suspect that the farce performed at Ludwigs-Universität was just a diversion.
You wonder... if perhaps the Kaiserinnen wanted to kill the Kurfürst?
Nein.
Ah, but you see, you can't hold back the birth of this thought.
If your father were standing here, what would he do?
My father...
He wouldn't do anything.
He would accept fate's plan, and turn away.
Even though he would go on to live with endless pain and regret.
And so, what about you? Would you... do the same?
He and I...
Frau Giallo?
Do not mistaken me for that distant relative of mine. There is no need for you to brandish your Arts Unit in my direction.
...This whole area is encircled by Leithanien's finest Gendarmerie.
Who are you, Herr Sankta?
I am an executo— I work with Michael.
Michael is a good boy. I guess... I can trust you.
That went surprisingly well.
At Michael's suggestion, I would like to provide a clue regarding Arturia.
What is it?
There is a high probability that she is planning an even more serious incident.
Seniora Viviana von Hochberg, it is my hope that we can cooperate, and put a stop to her plans.
Sorry, Frau Giallo. I made another mistake.
Why did you stop?
I don't think I played the last bar on the last line very well...
Your heart is in the right place. Don't let yourself be bound by the melody. Instead, guide it with your will.
I can't help but think about what I did wrong though.
Then don't hold yourself back.
Music is the voice of your heart. It's a language to express yourself with. If you let yourself be caught up in the grammar before learning how to speak, then language itself loses all meaning.
Your embarrassment, your self-doubt, and your thirst for the future... They're all real. So let them live in the sound of your instrument.
But that's not what my Arts professor said.
He said that Originium Arts is a rigorous field of study. That it's about uncovering the order within chaos, like putting together a mess of unrelated notes to form a beautiful chord.
But I'm having too many distracting thoughts, and my sound is all over the place. That's why I can't control the energy of my Arts well.
I taught another student just like you before. He was reserved, shy, and not very good at expressing himself.
Wh-What happened to him?
He became a very brave man.
A shame that he couldn't live the longer, more beautiful life I'd hoped he would.
But I was fortunate enough to hear his last performance. The cry of his instrument... was hardly inferior to the famed musician with whom he shared a surname.
Oh, was he the descendant of someone famous?
Herkunftshorn. Have you heard his work?
Who... did you just say?
People often call him the Witch King.
Th-That Witch King? *cough*... People hardly ever call him by name.
Really? But to me, he was first and foremost a musician.
Uh... Everyone says he stopped performing with regular instruments toward the end of his life.
His music... No, you could hardly call that noise music... They say it instilled fear in anyone who heard it.
True. His rhythm was in no way harmonious. Every last measure far exceeded what his audience imagined based on the previous one.
But I'd never felt such a strong... will.
You really shouldn't talk about this on campus.
I heard the Empresses' Voice and the Gendarmerie keep a close eye on Ludwigs-Universität. You'd be in deep trouble if they were to catch wind of it.
You should return to your dorm.
Keep practicing this piece. No matter what commotion you may hear outside, focus only on your own rhythm.
Arturia.
So it's you. If you're here to speak to me personally, then I take it the show is about to begin?
Almost. Our guests are arriving as they should, one by one.
Will you be performing everything that will happen today for me, just like you used to? I'd like to be present when the melody begins.
You wish to listen from beginning to end? I'm afraid that would be a very long piece.
A lot of people will be hurt today. Some will even die. They will be sacrificed because of me, and I cannot allow myself to look away.
Even though it hurts so much. You're far more determined than anyone.
You... When my performance reaches the end of your life, what will I see?
Ludwigs-Universität.
I've seen the name in more than a few books I've read. Most recently... in a leading publication on Originium Arts theory. But the name comes up as early as ancient scrolls engraved with secret Arts.
The crest of that university shines brilliantly in the history of Leithanien's Arts.
It's merely a variant of the Urtica family crest.
But it remains Leithanien's oldest university.
Once, I used to fantasize about life on a campus like this.
Walking through a campus covered with red leaves, surrounded by friends of similar ages, with an endless buffet of books and literature in the library... Every day must be fulfilling here.
With Their Majesties' permission, you are free to enroll in any school in Leithanien.
Oh... have you noticed the eyes of the students walking around us?
Their thirst for knowledge is enough for them to forget the divide between their upbringings.
Yet before Kurfürst Ludwig chose to make his spire a 'university,' all knowledge was locked away by decree.
So... how can I bring myself to use that very same authority to pollute such unique purity once more?
...The Gendarmerie?
Your so-called purity has never really existed.
Did you summon them here?
I wouldn't need to. The eyes up on the Spires have never looked away from Leithanien's lands.
And especially not from here. Before you lies the oldest spire in the Urtica territory. The Witch King stayed in it for a decade, and often returned even after his coronation.
The campus may seem peaceful from the outside, but every centimeter of this place can be traced back to the Witch King.
And that includes the man we're looking for.
Herr Gerhard Hoffmann?
Nein. We will find his teacher first. He's the one most familiar with Ludwigs-Universität.
Since the era of the Witch King... No, since an even more distant past, he's never left this school's most ancient spire.
...Why are there so many gendarmes on campus?
You said earlier that I was a wanted criminal.
You think they're here for you? No way... Besides, we've been very careful on the way. There's no way we got their attention.
Quick. Find a place to hide!
Team 5, Team 6, move up.
Team 4, search the whole spire. Do not let any suspicious characters through.
Stop! You can't just run around and use Arts like you own the place.
This is a university. A lot of the research we do here requires a stable environment.
Even the slightest disturbance could cause irreversible damage to the sophisticated equipment and the complicated Arts that have been cast.
Seize him.
Why? I didn't break any laws!
Your name.
Louis. Louis Schnitzler—You haven't answered my question!
The Schnitzlers of Fortsgard? Take him to the classrooms.
I don't understand...
Keep searching.
Take any commoners to the courtyard, and lock the nobles in the classrooms.
Take control of anyone and anything related to House Urtica.
I'm only a Schnitzler by name! My father didn't even inherit the title. What gives you the right to do all this over something that happened decades ago—
Because we're investigating what happened decades ago.
Young Herr Schnitzler. As long as you behave yourself, I assure you nothing will happen to you.
My family will be participating in the celebration soon! I'm going to have an audience with Their Majesties!
Ludwigs-Universität is the most historic university in Leithanian! Not even the Kaiserinnen's armies dared damage a single brick of this spire twenty-three years ago!
What gives you the right to trample on knowledge and the arts, on Leithanien's traditions?!
The Kaiserinnen's orders, young Herr.
The culprit who murdered our commander is hiding in this school.
If you want to protect it, then tell all your classmates that they'd better report anything they know about a strange Sankta and the Witch King's remains.
The Kaiserinnen's orders...?
No way...
You seem surprised.
Surprised? No, I'm just an idiot. Given her personality and disposition, how could I have thought she'd keep herself at arm's length?
I can't waste time chasing after Arturia anymore.
I thought she was the target of your mission too.
Only part of it. My... client wants to understand the reasoning behind her actions, in order to prevent her from getting in the way of our plans.
But now there's a bigger problem. I can't let this ruin the plan itself.
You...
I have no further questions. As you are Leithanian, I will not be required to execute your will.
Forget it. Not like I was expecting anything resembling a goodbye from you.
Herr Executor, the case Arturia is involved in is far more serious than you could ever imagine. Even your pope has no right to interfere.
Here's some advice: abandon your mission before the Gendarmerie make it here. Leave Zwillingstürme.
I've completed my assessment.
Abandoning this mission is not an option.
We've arrived, Droste. You'd best wait outside.
Hm? I sense no danger...
Don't move.
...What?
You're blocking my pipe. Yes, the one puffing out smoke to your left. If you move, I can't guarantee that your left hand will remain after the smoke ring passes.
You use a smoking pipe as a wand?

This? A wand? Don't be ridiculous. I smoke to relax. Who'd want to think about serious matters while smoking? I simply wanted run an experiment, and see if this smoke ring could float all the way to the top of the twin sheep's spire.
...We're here because we have important matters to discuss, Herr Fremont.
What a killjoy, Reiner. If only you were as well-behaved as the lovely little doe next to you.
I'd also be interested in seeing if a smoke ring could float for dozens of kilometers.
No, distance isn't the important factor. What matters is... not being blinded by that uselessly shiny armor of yours.
Alright, so you've ruined my mood. Now tell me, was it the gloomy sheep or the boastful sheep that asked you to come find me?
So you're telling me a copy of the Güldenesgesatz was lost. The one stored in Sturmwind.

Do we pay you to sit around all day?! You're the Gesatzswächter, and you can't even keep an eye on the Güldenesgesatz. What good is that fancy getup of yours, other than being hung from the top of the Spires as an ornament?
Seriously, that Grimmacht. 'It's safer to keep it far away from the center of whirlpool'? Apparently, some of your idiocy rubbed off on her! Maybe the bigger your horns, the stupider you get!
And don't get me started with that punk in Sturmwind! Honor his vow with his life? Like hell he did!
Kurfürst Werner has passed.
Before you stands his daughter.
It's a pleasure.
My condolences, if you want them. But that doesn't change the fact that this whole farce is the height of absurdity! Especially the fact that Grimmacht waited until now to tell me!
Is this stolen Güldenesgesatz really so special?
Twenty-three years ago, when Kurfürst Werner returned from Zwillingstürme to Sturmland, he had it on his person.
Aside from the Kaiserinnen and the Kurfürsten, no one wanted to be near it. My guess is that, like many of the Arts devices from the era of the Witch King, his Arts still remain in it.
The Witch King's Arts... The Witch King's Arts!
The entrance of Ludwigs-Universität has that fool's Arts, too. They could have all the armors lining the streets start breakdancing right this moment—but is there a point?
Did Grimmacht never mention it? Those are no normal, frivolous Arts. They're Arts devices that Herkunftshorn himself spent his life developing, all to defeat his greatest foe!
The Witch King's greatest foe... Do you mean Corsica I of Gaul?
A mere emperor, leading a mere army. Do you really think transient might like that would have meant a thing to Herkunftshorn in his heyday?
The enemy he defeated all those years ago was the will of Leithanien.
...The Güldenesgesatz.
Apparently you aren't a complete moron. Ever since the birth of the Leithanian Empire, the Güldenesgesatz has changed only once.
All on his own, Herkunftshorn issued a challenge to Leithanien's forebears, as well as to all the Arts masters who put their energy into composing the Güldenesgesatz.
How do you feel?
My head... hah... It doesn't hurt as much anymore.
I'm afraid normal healing Arts won't help you very much. Your pain isn't caused by physical injuries.
I know. There's an evil old fart shouting my ear off. That's all.
You... You can actually hear him speak?
Occasionally.
That must be unbearable. Like his melodies, the Witch King's voice holds a power that is difficult to endure.
If he wanted, he could kill a man simply by humming.
You've met him?
My teacher, Herr Fremont, was once the Witch King's closest friend.
When he sat atop the Witch King's Spire, there were once thousands of casters prostrating themselves before the steps to the throne. And I... I was one of them.

So you're also...
Ugh!
Is your head hurting again? Don't worry. I betrayed him in the end. I joined the resistance, and charged into the Spire under Grimmacht and Ewigegnade's leadership.
That day, the steps I once kneeled before were... redder than even the leaves in the Ludwigs-Universität campus.
It must have been a difficult battle.
For every ten companions I fought beside, only one survived... Every last one of us was among the most capable in the country.
If they had been as fortunate as me, they would have gone on to become even more outstanding scholars, even greater casters.
No wonder you visited Forest Park that day. I should salute you and those who sacrificed their lives.
Don't waste your strength... I'm not worth it.
If it hurts, though, feel free to lean on me.
Let him go.
Lessing...?
I knew you'd come after us.
Where's Professor Fremont?
He's very disappointed in you.
I'm sorry, Lessing. I don't want to hurt you, nor do I want to make an enemy of my dear teacher.
Then why stop us?
The Genesis Horn should not arrive like this.
We're not pursuing any evil causes. We're simply retrieving what belongs to us and destroying what hinders us. You know that.
The Witch King's relics... Who do they belong to? And who decides that?

You're a smart fellow, Lessing, and an honest one. You should think about these things, lest you regret all this in twenty or thirty years. I see you as my favorite student, which is why I don't want to see you make the same mistakes.
I once... saw you as a dear mentor too.
I still remember. You weren't even half as tall as you are now when Professor Fremont handed that sword to you.
Yet in the blink of an eye, you're as old as I was when I stormed the Witch King's Spire.
In school, time really does fly right by, doesn't it?
My fear is that not enough time has passed. That I'm not strong or worthy enough to raise my sword for those I want to protect.
I'm your target, remnant of the Witch King.
And I have only one thing to say to you: Get out of my way.
Classes are almost over.
Come, this way!
Maeyer, what are you doing standing here? Not auditing any lectures today?
Ahem... Why are you holding a sword in the school building? You really love training, don't you?
...He chose this path on purpose.
Have you heard? A large group of gendarmes barged into the school today. They're searching everyone they're coming across, especially commoners from the Urtica territory—
Hold on, why are you running so fast? Even up the stairs? The school administration doesn't allow that!
Are you okay? Why waste your strength on me? I'm much older than you. I should be the one protecting you.
Haa... Haa... Well, I'm the one he's after. Besides, I don't want to see any more blood spilled on my account.
Considering Lessing's abilities and temperament, he'll catch up to us soon.
We need to take a safer route.
Are we inside a school?
Ludwigs-Universität.
You are of the Urtica bloodline. If the Witch King hadn't fallen when you were born, you would have studied Arts here.
...I'd really wish I weren't.
The Urticas have produced more than one Leithanian ruler. Aside from Herkunftshorn and the other Kaisers...
Ludwig. Known as the Scholar, he was the Kurfürst of Einwald, and he too almost became the Kaiser of Leithanien. And he was also your ancestor.
He wanted everyone to 'gain the most supreme knowledge,' so that the land would 'never again impede Leithanien.'
When he insisted on opening up his family's spire to all of Einwald's citizens, many thought he'd gone mad.
Mad... Heh. I suppose a lot of our family get called that.
Madness goes hand-in-hand with greatness. This has always been the way of House Urtica. Even the Witch King was a benevolent ruler of unsurpassed talent, before he succumbed to his madness.
'Succumbing to madness' has far more frightening connotations when it happens to a tyrant rather than a commoner.
If that's another Urtica tradition, then I'll have to find somewhere to bleed out before it happens to me.
From the moment I laid eyes on you, I saw the bravery in you. But your life is precious, and self-destruction has never been the only path open to you.
Franz... May I call you Franz?
You know the name I threw away?
You won't ever be alone again. Let me help you.
...Gerhard Hoffmann.
Of all my students, he's the one who knows best what the Güldenesgesatz really is.
If only he were here. Then I wouldn't need to waste my energy explaining all this.
How did you know our purpose here?
Purpose?
The Witch King's remains killed my father, as well as two more who participated in the coup twenty-three years ago.
We suspect that their next target is Herr Hoffmann.
Hoh, you came at just the right time, then. Just fifteen minutes ago... Wait, what did you just say?
Gerhard is involved with the Witch King's remains? The same 'remains' that experimented on Herkunftshorn's great-grandson simply to blow up a dime-a-dozen music hall?
...I suppose so. They may not be the exact same group, but they're working under a similar banner.
But how do you know so much about even the finest details of the Vyseheim Incident?
This energy fluctuation... It's a sign that Arts were cast to maintain structural integrity here.
The same kind of Arts used by Einwald's caster army.
Don't mistake my Arts for their child's play.
Forget the Einwald of today, even when Herkunftshorn was still around, I was the one who taught all his lackeys their spatial Arts!
Stand back, Droste.
Why are you reaching for your blade? Stay away from my instruments. It's been centuries since this spire was last repaired, and it can't withstand being knocked around.
...Herr Fremont.
Is the experiment you were just conducting related to the Voice of Mundane?
Quick, upstairs!
I heard from a student just now. The Sankta we're looking for is hiding there!
A pipe organ.
The entire spire is designed in the shape of an enormous pipe organ. It matches the distinguishing characteristics of the background sounds in the cello piece we heard earlier.
There's a very high chance that the wanted criminal Arturia is hiding here.
Will you keep going up?
Originium Arts that use sculptures to synchronize voices. The very same kind I heard at the site of the attack at Karl Schmidt Street.
So you started working with the 'Witch King's remains' after all, Arturia.
If I told you... I never cared who they were, and had no interest in Leithanien's political turmoil, would you believe me?
The resentment of individuals has never been a motive for your crimes, and I see power struggles and profit as even less likely candidates.
Oh, Federico. You really do know me better than anyone. Everyone else's minds are tainted with thoughts, while you... boring as you are, you and I are alike.
All these years, I've walked amidst chaos simply to find an even stronger soul.
This journey was no different. I simply accepted the invitation of a few Leithanian acquaintances. That's the only reason for my visit.
Even without my assurances, you should already know in your heart that, no matter what becomes of Leithanien tomorrow, it will have no immediate effect on Laterano.
Perhaps.
Then, can I ask of you the same thing the Lupo boy did? That you ignore what you see here?
You're upstairs.
Hm. You guessed right. I suppose I could give you a reward and let you see me.
How about this? Once my wish comes true, I will be waiting for you at the city gates on the day I leave Zwillingstürme.
When the time comes, if you still want me to go back to Laterano with you, I promise you I'll think about it.
An arrest is not a request. You do not have the right to 'think about it.'
My lovely baby brother, you really... don't know when to give up.
Target spotted!
